findings.claim: Q1 rationale contains a non-verbatim passage quote.
findings.evidence: The Q1 WHY-KEY glosses the opening pitch in single quotes as 'cut the mean time to repair, put more ships and faster ships to sea', but the passage reads 'cut the mean time to repair, put more and faster ships to sea' (no inserted 'ships', no reorder). Verified by string search: the rationale's exact quoted string does not occur in the passage.
findings.why: Integrated review requires every quoted phrase in a rationale to appear verbatim in current passage/option text. This one does not. It is a dev-facing gloss of the same pitch already keyed correctly, so it does not affect any student-visible option, key, or answerability; the integrated verdict was already MINOR_NOTES, which this observation sits within.
